title: Forms, validation and accessible state

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Forms, validation and accessible state; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: DOM Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Forms, validation and accessible state
sources.claim: Form events should use submit at the form boundary, read named controls, validate, and preserve native keyboard submission.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Forms, validation and accessible state
sources.claim: Form events should use submit at the form boundary, read named controls, validate, and preserve native keyboard submission.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Form events should use submit at the form boundary, read named controls, validate, and preserve native keyboard submission.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A submit listener prevents navigation only after validation and disables duplicate requests while showing status. Your goal is to implement client validation as enhancement and verify keyboard focus and error announcement behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Scope, closures and modules; html project site; Event propagation and delegation; html forms validation. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Form events should use submit at the form boundary, read named controls, validate, and preserve native keyboard submission. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Click-only handling misses Enter submission, disabled fields are omitted, and async submission needs duplicate and error control. This draft therefore rejects: Handling the submit button's click is equivalent to handling every form submission.

includes: Use form events and constraint APIs while preserving labels, native submission and perceivable errors.
Implement client validation as enhancement and verify keyboard focus and error announcement behavior.
excludes: Click-only handling misses Enter submission, disabled fields are omitted, and async submission needs duplicate and error control.

outcomes: Implement client validation as enhancement and verify keyboard focus and error announcement behavior.

examples.title: Forms, validation and accessible state: concrete case
examples.situation: A submit listener prevents navigation only after validation and disables duplicate requests while showing status.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A submit listener prevents navigation only after validation and disables duplicate requests while showing status.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Listen for the form submit event so keyboard and programmatic submissions follow the same path.
examples.result: The result follows because Listen for the form submit event so keyboard and programmatic submissions follow the same path.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Handling the submit button's click is equivalent to handling every form submission.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Listen for the form submit event so keyboard and programmatic submissions follow the same path.

analogy.example: A coordinated event desk can model Forms, validation and accessible state, with values, tasks, callbacks, and external messages changing observable state.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Forms, validation and accessible state before using exact platform terms
limitations: Human coordination does not reproduce coercion, lexical scope, microtasks, prototypes, or browser security boundaries.
